heading: Strategy to respond to global bases of the People’s Republic of China
label: 7003 Strategy to respond to global bases of the People’s Republic of China
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026, and not as part of the U.S.-China Relations Act of 2000, which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Director of National Intelligence shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees an intelligence assessment analyzing the risk posed by PRC global bases and potential PRC global bases identified pursuant to subsection (b)(2)(A) to the ability of the United States or any United States ally to project power, maintain freedom of movement, and protect other interests.December 18, 2025

children.heading: Form
children.label: 2 Form
children.text: The assessment required in this subsection may be submitted in classified form.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of State, in coordination with the Secretary of Defense and other appropriate senior Federal officials, shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees the strategy described in paragraph (2).December 18, 2025

children.label: A
children.text: identify, of the locations where the PRC maintains or is suspected to be seeking a physical presence and that could transition into PRC global bases, not fewer than 5 locations that pose the greatest risk to the ability of the United States or any United States ally to project power, maintain freedom of movement, and protect other interests;

children.label: B
children.text: include a comprehensive list of executive branch entities involved in addressing aspects of PRC global base establishment, including estimated programmatic and personal resource requirements on an agency-by-agency basis to effectively address the issue of potential PRC global base establishment, and any relevant resource constraints;

children.label: C
children.text: describe in detail all executive branch efforts to mitigate the impacts to the national interests of the United States and partner countries of the locations described in subparagraph (A) and prevent the establishment of PRC global bases, including with resources described pursuant to subparagraph (B); and

children.label: D
children.text: for each of the locations identified pursuant to subparagraph (A), identify the actions by the United States or United States allies that would be most effective in ensuring that the government of the country in which such location is located does not host a PRC global base.
to implement such strategy to counter the PRC’s efforts to establish PRC global bases at the locations identified pursuant to subsection (b)(2)(A); and
to identify mitigation measures that would prevent the PRC from establishing PRC global bases in locations other than the locations identified pursuant to subsection (b)(2)(A).
conduct a review of the Executive Branch’s strategy and overall approach in response to efforts by the PRC to establish PRC global bases; and
submit to the appropriate congressional committees the results of such review and the updated information described in subsection (b)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate;

children.label: C
children.text: the Select Committee on Intelligence of the Senate;

children.label: D
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: E
children.text: the Committee on Foreign Affairs of the House of Representatives;

children.label: F
children.text: the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives;

children.label: G
children.text: the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: H
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: PRC
children.label: 2 PRC
children.text: The term “PRC” means the People’s Republic of China.

children.heading: PRC global base
children.label: 3 PRC global base
children.text: The term “PRC global base” means a physical location outside of the PRC where the PRC maintains an element of the People’s Liberation Army, PRC intelligence or security forces, or infrastructure designed to support the presence of PRC military, intelligence, or security forces, for the purposes of power projection.
